title: Math — Area & Volume
description: PSAT 8/9 geometry questions focused on area and volume (cylinders, circles, scaling, prisms, cubes, triangles).

questions.prompt: A manufacturing company produces two sizes of cylindrical containers that each have a height of 50 centimeters. The radius of container A is 16 centimeters, and the radius of container B is 25% longer than the radius of container A. What is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of container B?
questions.explanation: Container B has radius \(16(1.25)=20\) cm. Cylinder volume: \(V=\pi r^2 h\). So \(V=\pi(20^2)(50)=\pi(400)(50)=20{,}000\pi\).
choiceExplanations: Treats the radius incorrectly (e.g., not squaring the correct radius).
Common error: multiplying by 25 instead of using 25% increase and/or not squaring properly.
Common error: taking the radius as 25 instead of 20.

questions.prompt: Rectangles \(ABCD\) and \(EFGH\) are similar. The length of each side of \(EFGH\) is 6 times the length of the corresponding side of \(ABCD\). The area of \(ABCD\) is 54 square units. What is the area, in square units, of \(EFGH\)?
questions.explanation: If the linear scale factor is 6, the area scale factor is \(6^2=36\). So \(54\times 36=1{,}944\).
choiceExplanations: Would come from scaling area the wrong way (or using reciprocal thinking).
Equals \(6^2\) but forgets to multiply by the original area 54.
Equals \(54\times 6\) (linear scaling mistake).

questions.prompt: A right circular cylinder has a base diameter of 22 centimeters and a height of 6 centimeters. What is the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the cylinder?
questions.explanation: Radius is half the diameter: \(r=11\). Volume: \(V=\pi r^2 h=\pi(11^2)(6)=\pi(121)(6)=726\pi\).
choiceExplanations: Uses \(r=11\) but forgets to square (or uses \(\pi rh\)).
Common error: doubles something incorrectly (e.g., uses diameter in place of radius in one step).
Likely uses diameter 22 as radius: \(\pi(22^2)(6)=2{,}904\pi\).

questions.prompt: Circle A has a radius of \(3n\) and circle B has a radius of \(129n\), where \(n\) is a positive constant. The area of circle B is how many times the area of circle A?
questions.explanation: Circle area scales with the square of the radius. Ratio \(=\left(\frac{129n}{3n}\right)^2=(43)^2=1{,}849\).
choiceExplanations: Just the radius ratio \(129/3=43\), not the area ratio.
Unjustified linear scaling / arithmetic slip.
Uses the coefficient 129 without forming the ratio or squaring.

questions.prompt: A circle has a radius of 2.1 inches. The area of the circle is \(b\pi\) square inches, where \(b\) is a constant. What is the value of \(b\)?
A cube has a volume of 474,552 cubic units. What is the surface area, in square units, of the cube?
A right square prism has a height of 14 units. The volume of the prism is 2,016 cubic units. What is the length, in units, of an edge of the base?
questions.explanation: For a right square prism, \(V=s^2h\). So \(2{,}016=s^2(14)\Rightarrow s^2=144\Rightarrow s=12\) (length is positive).

questions.prompt: A right triangle has sides of length \(2\sqrt{2}\), \(6\sqrt{2}\), and \(\sqrt{80}\) units. What is the area of the triangle, in square units?
questions.explanation: The largest side is \(\sqrt{80}=4\sqrt{5}\), so it is the hypotenuse. The legs are \(2\sqrt{2}\) and \(6\sqrt{2}\). Area \(=\frac12(2\sqrt{2})(6\sqrt{2})=\frac12(12\cdot 2)=12\).
choiceExplanations: Adds lengths (perimeter-like), not area.
Correct: \(\frac12(2\sqrt2)(6\sqrt2)=12\).
Expression is not consistent with \(\frac12 bh\) and is far too large.
Common overestimate (e.g., forgetting the \(\tfrac12\) and/or mishandling radicals).

questions.prompt: The length of each edge of a box is 29 inches. Each side of the box is in the shape of a square. The box does not have a lid. What is the exterior surface area, in square inches, of this box without a lid?
questions.explanation: A lidless cube-like box has 5 square faces. Each face area \(=29^2=841\). Exterior surface area \(=5\times 841=4{,}205\).

questions.prompt: A cube has a surface area of 54 square meters. What is the volume, in cubic meters, of the cube?
questions.explanation: Surface area \(=6s^2\). So \(54=6s^2\Rightarrow s^2=9\Rightarrow s=3\). Volume \(=s^3=27\).
choiceExplanations: Common arithmetic error after solving for \(s\).
Correct: \(s=3\Rightarrow s^3=27\).
Often from using \(s^2\) instead of \(s^3\) or mixing area/volume.
Overestimate from using \(54\) too directly (e.g., treating 54 as \(s^3\)).
